attacking_style: Rarely initiates attacks; primarily responds to moves by others but struggles to sustain counterattacks in final kilometers. Excels in sustained climbing TT efforts rather than explosive accelerations.
team_tactics: Frequently isolated without team support in critical moments; often riding independently or reduced to following wheels without domestique protection, particularly evident in 2023 Vuelta and multiple Grand Tour stages.
terrain_preference: Dominant on long, steady-gradient climbing TTs (won Romandie ITT by 30+ seconds). Vulnerable on punchy accelerations and when early aggressive pacing is set on first-category climbs under 15km.
vulnerability: Illness susceptibility (abandoned Giro 2023 twice while ill); struggles when pace lifts early on climbs; poor final-kilometer acceleration when positioned well but lacks snap to respond to attacks under 3.5km.
key_patterns: Repeatedly dropped when early aggressive tempo set on climbs, not explosive attacks (4 instances).
Strong positioning instincts in flat stages/early race but isolated in mountains without support (3 instances).
Better form trajectory within stage races; improves as race progresses rather than peaking early (3 instances).
Unable to match final-kilometer accelerations despite being well-positioned in closing kilometers (4 instances).
Illness/health management issues cost significant GC opportunities, particularly post-rest days (3 instances).
form_trajectory: negative: Recent 2023 data shows concerning pattern of early-race weakness (dropped Tour de Suisse opener), illness abandonments (Giro twice), and complete team isolation by Vuelta. Decline from 2021-2022 GC competitiveness.
built_from: 2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 18
